ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for doner wrap; mapped to closest parent class 'sandwich (food product)' as a flatbread-wrapped meat dish. FoodOn does not have a dedicated doner wrap or kebab wrap term as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwich, wrap) is the closest defensible match for a flatbread-wrapped kebab product. No dedicated doner wrap code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for doner wrap as a food product. SNOMED CT food hierarchy does not include this specific dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard doner wrap serving is approximately 250 g, comprising a flatbread filled with seasoned doner meat, tomato, onion, and cucumber, typically served with fries on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Doner Wrap — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Doner Wrap (cr_id G00010169). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for flatbread-wrapped mixed meat and vegetable dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for doner wrap as a composite dish. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: refined wheat flatbread (pita/lavash), which has a published GI of approximately 57–68 in the University of Sydney GI Database (e.g., pita bread, white: GI ~57; Lebanese flatbread: GI ~75; average ~65). The presence of protein (14.7 g/100g) and fat (12.4 g/100g) from doner meat moderates the overall glycaemic response, supporting a composite dish GI estimate of 60. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 56.5) / 100 = 33.9, where carbohydrate per 250g serving = 22.61 × 2.5 = 56.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 562 mg/100g; a 250g serving delivers approximately 1405 mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit doner wrap consumption to occasional intake, request reduced salt in meat seasoning, avoid additional high-sodium sauces such as chilli or garlic sauce, and pair with potassium-rich foods to partially offset sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 22.6 g/100g from refined flatbread with low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100g) produces a medium-to-high glycaemic load of approximately 33.9 per standard serving, which can elevate postprandial blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should request a wholemeal or high-fibre wrap to reduce the glycaemic load, limit portion size to half a wrap, increase the proportion of salad vegetables, and monitor postprandial glucose response after consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 4.5 g/100g, trans fat 0.1 g/100g, cholesterol 42 mg/100g, and sodium 562 mg/100g collectively elevate cardiovascular risk through LDL-raising and blood-pressure-raising mechanisms.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose chicken doner over lamb to reduce saturated fat and arachidonic acid intake, avoid creamy or high-fat sauces, limit frequency to once per week, and ensure the rest of the day's diet is low in saturated fat and sodium.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat doner (lamb) is a source of arachidonic acid promoting inflammatory cytokines; trans fat 0.1 g/100g and saturated fat ~4.5 g/100g further drive joint inflammation; sodium 562 mg/100g exceeds the RA-recommended threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should choose chicken doner as a lower arachidonic acid alternative, monitor individual response to tomato (nightshade), and minimise frequency of consumption given the pro-inflammatory fat and sodium profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Doner Wrap is a Middle Eastern-origin street food dish popular in Singapore and globally, consisting of seasoned doner meat — either chicken or lamb — cooked on a vertical rotisserie and sliced, then wrapped in a soft flatbread (typically pita or lavash) together with fresh tomato, sliced onion, and cucumber. The dish is commonly served with fries. The flatbread provides a soft, slightly chewy exterior while the doner meat contributes a savoury, spiced, and slightly smoky flavour profile. Per 100g, the dish delivers 262 kcal, 14.7g protein, 12.4g fat, and 22.6g carbohydrate, with sodium at 562mg — placing it in the high-sodium category. Dietary fibre is low at 1.4g/100g, reflecting the use of refined flatbread. The dish has a medium estimated glycaemic index of approximately 60, moderated by the protein and fat content of the meat. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease, while the moderate glycaemic load (approximately 33.9 per 250g serving) warrants caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat (~4.5g/100g) and trace trans fat (0.1g/100g) are relevant for cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis risk. Calcium is notably present at 188mg/100g, likely from the flatbread and meat preparation. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified meat and is a common lunch and dinner option across Middle Eastern restaurants and kebab stalls in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Doner Wrap (cr_id G00010169) with a stated confidence of 0.95. All core macros and key micronutrients are present and internally consistent. Source match is exact.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite doner wrap dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this dish as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g estimated at 4.5g/100g based on typical doner meat and flatbread composition (USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for lamb kebab and pita bread); this field is not explicitly reported in the HPB source. trans_fat_g estimated at 0.1g/100g consistent with the input health_suitability notes referencing trace trans fat; not explicitly reported in HPB source. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on the refined flatbread carbohydrate vehicle moderated by protein and fat from doner meat; no direct GI measurement for doner wrap exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycaemic load per serving = (60 × 56.5) / 100 = 33.9, based on 250g serving size. The dish is classified as halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified meat, which is standard practice in Singapore kebab outlets. Fries are listed as a side component in the description but nutrient values reflect the wrap only as reported by HPB.
